subject: Re: LSJ projects 5 November 2006
body:
sugar mill to start this week  , no later than wed...the mbr terrace has to be thoughtful not backhoed, new carpet for main terraces, Clean top of hill and flag pole,, stones gone as well as bottles, heli pad ,, is all done in house   no budget needed,, except for cement,,, ,13, location of date to be tomorrow.5 big bismarcks,, heli road repositioned and stones continued

----- Original Message ----
From: "Miles Alexander, LSJ" <littlestjames@yahoo.com>
To: Jeffery E Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Cc: Emad Hanna <ehanna@nysgmail.com>; Daphne Wallace <daphnew@ftcvi.com>
Sent: Sunday, November 5, 2006 4:22:32 PM
Subject: LSJ projects 5 November 2006

Greetings
  The following are projects that JE has requested.
   
  1.MBR - Clear bush on the lower side of the service road so that viewing from MBR terrace will not be interrupted. Brinsley
  2. Pool area - Remove two large white pots. Miles
  3. General clean up - Perform a "chicken parade" around the island to pick up trash, bottles plastic etc. Miles
  4. Gym. Rework landscaping to improve the area. Focus on pots and agave plants. Julia
  5. Gym. Investigate and research square paving stones that are on large mesh supports (Similar to mosaic tiles).  If acceptable by JE, remove gravel and pave. Miles/Emad
  6. Small salt pond area.  Once planted out, JE wants more grass around salt pond. Brinsley
  7. Blue shingle shed. Plant out area. Brinsley
  8. Helipad steps. Pour cement slab and finish with tile and pebbles. Rework road. Miles to prepare
 budget for approval.
  9. Iguana corner road. Re-bank road once wall work is completed. Miles
  10. EDSA company to visit island on 13th. Ask them for ideas regarding spindle palms. Brinsley
  11. Move bismark plants from terrace road to bismark planter near tiki house.
  12. Remove all marked coconuts from owners road and plant on beach. JE to advise placement.
  13. Move date palm from Iguana corner and replant on owners road. 
  14. Rework tall tree plantation. JE to advise locations.
  15. Main Parking area. Build stone walls and window to mimic an old sugar mill. JE has advised location of window, height of walls and finished look required. This project will start once the terrace road is complete. Miles to work up budget for approval.
  16. Parking area. Install flood lights on lower side of wall as well as two lanterns on either side of the existing window. Miles
  17. View
 site shelters. Miles to build one sample at the viewing site between beach two and three. Dimensions are 16 * 16 with a maximum entrance height of 7 feet. Roof is to be standard hip with metal roofing sheets. Pillars are square using blocks.
  Floor to be tiled. Miles to create budget for approval.
  18. Additional pre fabricated shelters. JE wants info/catalogs etc from the following places. classicrecreation.com, portercorp.com, vixenhill.com. Miles/Emad
   
  Thanks
   
  Miles


    Mr Miles Alexander 
  Little St James Island, 6100 Red Hook Qrts B3, St Thomas, USVI, USA. 00802-1348
  Phone : 340-5139855, Fax : 340-7758108, Email : littlestjames@yahoo.com
body_html:
sugar mill to start this week  , no later than wed...the mbr terrace has to be thoughtful not backhoed, new carpet for main terraces, Clean top of hill and flag pole,, stones gone as well as bottles, heli pad ,, is all done in house   no budget needed,, except for cement,,, ,13, location of date to be tomorrow.5 big bismarcks,, heli road repositioned and stones continued

----- Original Message ----
From: "Miles Alexander, LSJ" <littlestjames@yahoo.com>
To: Jeffery E Epstein <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Cc: Emad Hanna <ehanna@nysgmail.com>; Daphne Wallace <daphnew@ftcvi.com>
Sent: Sunday, November 5, 2006 4:22:32 PM
Subject: LSJ projects 5 November 2006

Greetings
The following are projects that JE has requested.
 
1.MBR - Clear bush on the lower side of the service road so that viewing from MBR terrace will not be interrupted. Brinsley
2. Pool area - Remove two large white pots. Miles
3. General clean up - Perform a "chicken parade" around the island to pick up trash, bottles plastic etc. Miles
4. Gym. Rework landscaping to improve the area. Focus on pots and agave plants. Julia
5. Gym. Investigate and research square paving stones that are on large mesh supports (Similar to mosaic tiles).  If acceptable by JE, remove gravel and pave. Miles/Emad
6. Small salt pond area.  Once planted out, JE wants more grass around salt pond. Brinsley
7. Blue shingle shed. Plant out area. Brinsley
8. Helipad steps. Pour cement slab and finish with tile and pebbles. Rework road. Miles to prepare budget for approval.
9. Iguana corner road. Re-bank road once wall work is completed. Miles
10. EDSA company to visit island on 13th. Ask them for ideas regarding spindle palms. Brinsley
11. Move bismark plants from terrace road to bismark planter near tiki house.
12. Remove all marked coconuts from owners road and plant on beach. JE to advise placement.
13. Move date palm from Iguana corner and replant on owners road.
14. Rework tall tree plantation. JE to advise locations.
15. Main Parking area. Build stone walls and window to mimic an old sugar mill. JE has advised location of window, height of walls and finished look required. This project will start once the terrace road is complete. Miles to work up budget for approval.
16. Parking area. Install flood lights on lower side of wall as well as two lanterns on either side of the existing window. Miles
17. View site shelters. Miles to build one sample at the viewing site between beach two and three. Dimensions are 16 * 16 with a maximum entrance height of 7 feet. Roof is to be standard hip with metal roofing sheets. Pillars are square using blocks.
Floor to be tiled. Miles to create budget for approval.
18. Additional pre fabricated shelters. JE wants info/catalogs etc from the following places. classicrecreation.com, portercorp.com, vixenhill.com. Miles/Emad
 
Thanks
 
Miles


Mr Miles Alexander
Little St James Island, 6100 Red Hook Qrts B3, St Thomas, USVI, USA. 00802-1348
Phone : 340-5139855, Fax : 340-7758108, Email : littlestjames@yahoo.com
